caption: Windows Service Activity
description: Windows Service Activity events report when a process interacts with the Service Control Manager.
name: windows_service_activity

activity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event. Each event class defines its own set of activity values. Use 0 (Unknown) when the activity cannot be determined. Use 99 (Other) when the activity does not match any defined value, in which case activity_name must be populated with the source-specific label.
Refer to the win_service attribute for details, unless any other attribute is mentioned.

1.caption: Create
1.description: A service is created, for example by calling the CreateService API.

2.caption: Reconfigure
2.description: A service is reconfigured, for example by calling the ChangeServiceConfig or ChangeServiceConfig2 API. Refer to prev_win_service and win_service attributes for details.

3.caption: Start
3.description: A stopped service is started, for example by calling the StartService API.

4.caption: Stop
4.description: A running or paused service is stopped, for example by calling the ControlService or ControlServiceEx API.

5.caption: Pause
5.description: A running service is paused, for example by calling the ControlService or ControlServiceEx API.

6.caption: Continue
6.description: A paused service is continued, for example by calling the ControlService or ControlServiceEx API.

7.caption: Delete
7.description: A service is deleted, for example by calling the DeleteService API.

prev_win_service.description: The Windows service before the mutation.

win_service.description: The current Windows service. On failed or unknown status of Reconfigure action may be populated with the intended state.
